info.title: Holt API
info.description: Complete HTTP API for the Holt chat platform (Keel backend). All paths are relative to the /api/v1 base. Message and key payloads are end-to-end encrypted client-side; see the bot API page for the crypto details.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Account bootstrap, login, and device linking.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: The authenticated user (/me): profile, status, sessions, blocks.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: Send, read, edit, delete, ack, typing.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Channel webhooks and their public execution endpoint.

tags.name: Slash commands
tags.description: Bot slash commands: list available commands for a channel and fire interactions.

get.description: Public bootstrap endpoint. Returns instance running marker, server version, and configuration including limits and feature flags (calls, webhooks, presence, typing, push with VAPID public key, legal docs presence). 'password_protected' indicates whether signup requires an instance password. Includes 'dev: true' when the server runs in dev mode.

dev.description: Present and true only in dev mode

get.description: Public endpoint to check whether a username is available before signup. Returns success when free, or a 400 error when already in use.

400.description: Username is in use

post.description: Public endpoint. Validates the username and RSA public key, then issues an encrypted challenge that must be solved via /solve to create the account. If the instance is password protected, a matching 'password' form field is required.

password.description: Required only when the instance is password protected

400.description: Username is in use

post.description: Public endpoint. Verifies username, passkey (bcrypt) and that the supplied public key matches the stored one, then issues an encrypted challenge to be solved via /solve to obtain a session.

401.description: Invalid login details or public key mismatch

post.description: Public endpoint. Submits the decrypted solution to a challenge issued by /signup, /login or /reset-passkey. On success it completes the relevant flow: creating an account (returns session + passkey), logging in (returns session), or resetting a passkey (returns new passkey). A session row is created for signup/login flows.

200.description: Challenge solved. Returns session for login/signup flows (with passkey on new signup), or new passkey for reset-passkey flow.

passkey.description: Returned on new signup or reset-passkey

400.description: Invalid challenge ID, username in use, or user not found

post.description: Authenticated endpoint. Verifies the supplied public key matches the logged-in user's stored key, then issues an encrypted challenge to be solved via /solve, which generates and returns a new passkey.

400.description: User not found

401.description: Validation failed, unauthorized, or public key mismatch

post.description: Authenticated endpoint. Creates a pending device link and returns a short-lived code (5 minute expiry) that another device can claim to be authorized onto this account.
Public endpoint used by the new (unauthenticated) device. Submits its public key against a device link code, moving the link to 'claimed' status so the existing logged-in device can approve it.

public.description: RSA public key of the claiming device

400.description: Invalid code or code already claimed

get.description: Authenticated endpoint used by the existing device to poll a device link. Returns the link status plus the claiming device's public key and user agent details once claimed. Returns 'expired' if the code is unknown or expired.

post.description: Authenticated endpoint. Approves a claimed device link, creating a new session for the account, RSA-encrypting it to the claiming device's public key, and storing an opaque blob for the new device to retrieve via /devicelink/result.

blob.description: Opaque encrypted blob handed to the new device

400.description: Invalid code, not ready to approve, or user not found

post.description: Authenticated endpoint. Marks a device link as rejected so the claiming device is denied. Fails if the link was already approved.

400.description: Invalid code or code already approved

get.description: Public endpoint polled by the new device. Returns the current status; once approved, returns the RSA-encrypted session, blob, username and account public key, and consumes (deletes) the link. Returns 'expired' if unknown or expired.

200.description: Device link result. Includes session/blob/account details only when status is 'approved'.

400.description: User not found

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's account info. May respond 419 with a re-authentication challenge when the session's next_challenge interval has elapsed; the client must solve the challenge to continue.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's display name and/or profile picture. Send `remove_pfp=1` to remove the current picture. At least one valid parameter must be provided.

display.description: New display name; empty string clears it, otherwise must be between 2 and 24 characters.

remove_pfp.description: Send "1" to remove the current profile picture. Ignored if `pfp` is also present.

400.description: No valid parameters to update

delete.description: Permanently deletes the authenticated user's account, leaving all channels and cleaning up associated files and keys. If the user is the sole owner of non-empty channels, the ?force query flag is required to delete those channels.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Presence of this flag forces deletion of channels where the user is the last owner.

403.description: Last owner of non-empty channels; ?force required

delete.description: Deletes the current session, logging the authenticated user out of this device.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's presence status and sharing preferences. Requires presence to be enabled on the server. At least one valid parameter must be provided.

400.description: Invalid or missing parameters

get.description: Returns all active sessions for the authenticated user, ordered newest first. The current session is flagged.

delete.description: Deletes all sessions for the authenticated user, logging out every device.
Deletes a specific session belonging to the authenticated user.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: ID of the session to delete.

get.description: Returns the list of users blocked by the authenticated user, ordered most recently blocked first.

post.description: Blocks the user with the given username. Cannot block yourself or an already blocked user.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the user to block.

400.description: Cannot block yourself

delete.description: Removes a block on the user with the given username. The target must currently be blocked.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the user to unblock.

404.description: User not found or not blocked

get.description: Returns all channels the current user is a member of (and has not hidden). Each channel has a `type`: 1=DM, 2=group, 3=broadcast. Includes unread counts, member counts and a preview of the last message. For broadcast channels (type 3) the last-message author is hidden from members without send/manage permissions.

post.description: Creates a channel. `type` controls the kind: 1=DM (requires `target_user`, reuses/unhides an existing DM if present), 2=group or 3=broadcast (both require `name`). An optional `pfp` image may be uploaded. Fails with 403 if channel creation is disabled or the per-user channel limit is reached.

target_user.description: Required for type=1 (DM). Username of the DM target.

400.description: Missing or invalid parameters

403.description: Creation disabled, channel limit reached, blocked, or private-bot DM not allowed

patch.description: Updates a non-DM channel's `name`, `permissions` (base permissions, masked) and/or `pfp`. Requires the manage-channel permission; modifying `permissions` additionally requires the manage-permissions permission. DM channels (type 1) cannot be modified.

400.description: No valid parameters to update, or DM channel

delete.description: For DM channels (type 1) this hides the channel for the current user. For group/broadcast channels it removes the current user as a member; if `?delete` is passed and the user is an owner the whole channel is deleted. The last owner cannot leave a non-empty channel. Fails with 403 if channel deletion is disabled.

parameters.name: delete
parameters.description: If present and the user is an owner, deletes the entire channel rather than leaving.

403.description: Deletion disabled or last owner cannot leave

get.description: Returns the current invite code for a non-DM channel (or null if none). Requires the manage-channel permission. DM channels (type 1) do not support invites.

400.description: DM channels do not support invites

post.summary: Create or set the channel invite code
post.description: Generates a new invite code for a non-DM channel, or sets a custom `invite_code`. Requires the manage-channel permission. Fails with 409 if the custom code is already in use by another channel. DM channels (type 1) do not support invites.

invite_code.description: Optional custom invite code; a random 8-char code is generated if omitted.

400.description: Invalid invite code or DM channel

delete.description: Clears the invite code for a non-DM channel. Requires the manage-channel permission. DM channels (type 1) do not support invites.

400.description: DM channels do not support invites

get.description: Returns public information about the channel an invite code points to, including name, pfp, `type` (1=DM, 2=group, 3=broadcast), member count and whether the current user is already a member.

post.description: Joins the channel referenced by the invite code. Bots cannot join themselves. Fails if already a member, banned, the per-user channel limit is reached, or (for non-broadcast channels) the channel is at its member cap.

400.description: Already a member or channel at member limit

403.description: Bots cannot join, banned, or channel limit reached

post.summary: Add a bot to a channel
post.description: Adds a bot to a non-DM channel (Discord-style: bots never join themselves; a member with the manage-members permission adds a bot they own). Optional `permissions` are granted only for bits the caller themselves holds; private bots can only be added by their owner. Bots are exempt from the channel member cap.

bot.description: Bot id or username (a leading @ is stripped).

permissions.description: Optional permission bitmask to grant the bot (limited to bits the caller holds).

400.description: DM channel or bot already in channel

403.description: Manage-members required, not owner of private bot, or bot banned

get.description: Lists members of a channel. Pass `?pb=true` to return only usernames and public keys (for encryption); for broadcast channels (type 3) this is limited to members with manage-members/manage-permissions. Without `pb`, returns a paginated member list; permission bitmasks are only included for callers with the manage-permissions permission. Broadcast channels require manage-members/manage-permissions to view members at all.

parameters.name: pb
parameters.description: If true, returns username + public key pairs instead of the full member list.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page (ignored when pb=true).

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Pagination page size (ignored when pb=true).

200.description: Member list (full members, or username/public-key pairs when pb=true)

delete.description: Removes a member from a non-DM channel. Requires the manage-members permission. You cannot kick yourself; only owners may kick owners or admins. DM channels (type 1) do not support kicking.

400.description: Cannot kick yourself or DM channel

403.description: Member management privileges required or target outranks caller

patch.description: Sets a member's permission bitmask in a non-DM channel. Requires the manage-permissions permission. Non-owners cannot assign permission bits they don't hold or modify owners/admins; the last owner cannot remove their own owner permission. DM channels (type 1) are not supported.

permissions.description: New permission bitmask (masked) for the target member.

400.description: Missing/invalid permissions or DM channel

403.description: Insufficient permissions, cannot assign unheld bits, or last owner

get.description: Returns messages in a channel newer than the member's join sequence, ordered newest-first. In non-broadcast channels the message `content` and `iv` are E2E-encrypted (the AES key is referenced by `key` and fetched separately). In broadcast channels (type 3) without send/manage permission, author info and signatures are hidden.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Upper bound timestamp (exclusive unless paired with after).

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Lower bound timestamp (exclusive unless paired with before).

parameters.name: before_message_id
parameters.description: Return messages before this message's sequence.

parameters.name: after_message_id
parameters.description: Return messages after this message's sequence.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filter by author id (20 chars, or '0' for webhooks).

400.description: Invalid parameter

post.description: Sends a message to a channel. Request is multipart/form-data with fields: `content`, `timestamp`, `signature`, `key`, `iv`, `nonce`, `replied_to`, `files`, and (bots only) `components`. In non-broadcast channels the `content` and `iv` are E2E-encrypted and `key` must reference the channel's current AES key id; `iv` must be 16 chars. The `signature`/`timestamp` are verified against a signing window. Attachments may be individually encrypted via per-file `attachments_meta`. Bots may include a `components` field (JSON-encoded array of action rows) to attach interactive buttons to the message. `embed_asset_ids` (JSON array of file ids from the embed-asset upload endpoint) confirms ownership of any embed image/thumbnail/icon references used in this message's content so they are not garbage-collected.

content.description: E2E-encrypted message body (plaintext in broadcast channels). May contain `! embed: {json}`, `! diagram: {json}` (Mermaid syntax, key `code`), and `! interactive: {json}` (sandboxed HTML/CSS/JS, keys `html`/`css`/`js`) blocks, each on their own line; clients render these specially. See the Bot API guide's Embeds, Diagrams, and Interactive content sections for the block formats.

timestamp.description: Signed client timestamp, validated against the signature window.

signature.description: Signature over the message and timestamp.

key.description: Current channel AES key id (required in non-broadcast channels).

replied_to.description: Message id this message replies to.

components.description: Bots only. JSON-encoded array of up to 5 action rows containing buttons. Pass a JSON string, e.g. '[{"type":1,"components":[...]}]'.

embed_asset_ids.description: JSON-encoded array of up to 40 file ids previously returned by POST /channel/{channel_id}/embed-asset, referenced by any embed image/thumbnail/icon_url in this message's content.

400.description: Invalid request

403.description: No permission / blocked / no encryption key

patch.description: Edits a message you authored. Multipart/form-data with `content`, `timestamp`, `signature`, and (in non-broadcast channels) a 16-char `iv`. `content` is optional when bots provide `components`. The `content`/`iv` remain E2E-encrypted; the new `signature`/`timestamp` are re-validated against the signing window. Bots may also pass `components` (JSON-encoded) to update interactive buttons.

400.description: Invalid request

403.description: Can only edit your own messages

delete.description: Deletes a message. Authors can delete their own; otherwise the manage_messages permission is required. Cleans up unused files and unused E2E keys.

post.description: Marks the channel as read up to its latest message, updating the caller's read marker.

404.description: Channel not found or no messages

post.description: Broadcasts a typing signal in DM (type 1) or group (type 2) channels. Suppressed if typing indicators are disabled, the user is invisible, has share_typing off, or is blocked.

400.description: Unsupported channel type

get.description: Returns the id of the channel's current (non-expired) E2E AES key, or null if none. Requires send_messages permission. Broadcast channels (type 3) do not use E2EE.

400.description: Broadcast channels don't use E2EE

post.description: Rotates the channel's E2E key. Body is a JSON object mapping every channel member's username to the RSA-wrapped AES key for that member (each wrapped key must be 344 chars). Every member must be present. Requires send_messages permission; broadcast channels (type 3) are rejected. The new key expires 24h later.

schema.description: Map of member username to RSA-wrapped AES key for that member.

400.description: Invalid payload / key length / members

get.description: Returns the caller's RSA-wrapped AES key entry for the given key id, including who rotated it and its expiry. Decryptable only with the caller's private key.

post.description: Returns the caller's RSA-wrapped AES key entries for a list of key ids (max 100). Each entry is decryptable only with the caller's private key. Body is a JSON array of key ids.

schema.description: List of key ids to fetch.

400.description: Invalid payload / too many keys

get.description: Returns a paginated list of users banned from the channel. Requires the manage members permission. Not available for DM channels.

400.description: Cannot manage bans for DM channels

post.description: Bans the target user from the channel and removes their membership. Cannot ban yourself, owners, or admins unless you are an owner. Requires the manage members permission.

400.description: Invalid request (DM channel or cannot ban yourself)

delete.description: Removes an existing ban for the target user. Requires the manage members permission. Not available for DM channels.

400.description: Cannot manage bans for DM channels

404.description: Channel, user, or ban not found

get.description: Returns the current call status for the channel, including participants. Part of the WebRTC signaling flow. Participant identifiers are only exposed if the requester is an active participant.

post.description: Starts a new call in the channel or joins an existing one as part of the WebRTC signaling flow. Leaves any other active call first. Fails if calls are disabled, the channel does not support calls, the call is full, or the user is blocked in a DM.

400.description: Calls not supported or already in call

403.description: Calls disabled, call full, or blocked

delete.description: Leaves the active call in the channel as part of the WebRTC signaling flow. The call is ended when the last participant leaves.

400.description: Already left this call

404.description: Channel not found or not in call

post.description: Relays a WebRTC signaling message (offer, answer, ice, or settings) to another participant in the call. A target participant is required for all types except settings. The caller must be an active participant.

target.description: Target participant user id (required unless type is settings)

400.description: Invalid signal type or missing target

403.description: You are not in this call

get.description: Returns all bots owned by the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new bot owned by the authenticated user and returns the bot along with a one-time 30-character authentication token. The token is shown only once. Fails if the bot limit is reached or the username is in use.

400.description: Invalid parameters or username in use

get.description: Returns the authenticated bot's own information. Only available when authenticated with a bot token.

403.description: This endpoint is only available to bots

patch.description: Updates an owned bot's privacy flag, display name, or profile picture. Send `remove_pfp=1` to remove the current picture. At least one valid field must be provided.

remove_pfp.description: Send "1" to remove the current profile picture. Ignored if `pfp` is also present.

400.description: No valid parameters to update

delete.description: Permanently deletes an owned bot and cleans up its unused files and keys.

post.description: Revokes the bot's existing token and returns a new one-time 30-character authentication token.

get.description: Lists all webhooks configured for a broadcast channel. Requires the caller to be a member with the manage_channel permission. Webhooks are only supported in broadcast channels (type 3). Each returned webhook includes its execution URL.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: ID of the broadcast channel

403.description: Webhooks disabled or missing management privileges

post.description: Creates a new webhook for a broadcast channel and returns the webhook, its secret token, and the ready-to-use send URL. Requires the caller to be a member with the manage_channel permission. Webhooks are only supported in broadcast channels (type 3).

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: ID of the broadcast channel

pfp.description: Optional image data URI (png/jpeg/jpg/webp/gif/x-icon) used as the webhook avatar

token.description: Secret token used to execute the webhook

send_url.description: Execution URL including the token query parameter

400.description: Invalid payload (bad name length or invalid pfp)

403.description: Webhooks disabled or missing management privileges

post.description: Public, unauthenticated endpoint that posts a message into a broadcast channel using a webhook. Authentication is performed solely with the webhook token supplied as the `token` query parameter (no bearer auth). The `service` path segment selects a compatibility mode for the request body and is case-insensitive; accepted values are `discord` and `github`. With `discord`, the body follows Discord's webhook shape (`content`, optional `embeds`, `username`, `avatar_url`); with `github`, the GitHub event is read from the `X-GitHub-Event` header and rendered into a text message (supports ping, push, pull_request, issues, issue_comment, release, with a generic fallback). Any unrecognized service value is rejected. The generic (no-service) form of this endpoint accepts `content`, `name`, and `pfp`. Message content is truncated to the configured maximum length.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: ID of the broadcast channel

parameters.name: webhook_id
parameters.description: ID of the webhook to execute

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Compatibility mode for the payload (case-insensitive): discord or github

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Webhook secret token used to authenticate the execution

parameters.name: X-GitHub-Event
parameters.description: GitHub event name, used only when service is github

embeds.description: Discord-style embeds, serialized into content if content is empty (discord mode)

avatar_url.description: Override webhook avatar as image data URI (discord mode)

pfp.description: Override webhook avatar as image data URI (generic mode)

400.description: Invalid payload, unsupported compatibility mode, missing content, or not a broadcast channel

post.description: Registers or updates a Web Push subscription for the authenticated user. If a subscription with the same endpoint already exists it is updated, otherwise a new one is created. Content is never included in push payloads since messages are end-to-end encrypted.

400.description: Invalid subscription

delete.description: Deletes the authenticated user's Web Push subscription identified by its endpoint.

endpoint.description: Push service endpoint URL to remove

400.description: Invalid endpoint

get.description: Public, unauthenticated endpoint returning a legal document as both raw Markdown and rendered HTML. The `doc` path parameter must be one of terms, privacy, or rules; any other value or a missing document returns 404.

parameters.name: doc
parameters.description: Which legal document to fetch

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of realtime events for the authenticated user. Returns a `text/event-stream` response that stays open and emits events as named SSE messages, interleaved with `: heartbeat` comments. Because the EventSource API cannot set headers, authentication is supplied via the `authorization=Bearer <token>` query parameter rather than the Authorization header (hence `security: []` here; this endpoint still requires a valid token through that query param). On connection the stream first replays a snapshot of active calls and visible presence. Emitted event names: message_sent, message_edited, message_deleted, member_join, member_leave, member_perms_changed, channel_added, channel_edited, channel_deleted, typing, presence_update, presence_remove, call_start, call_join, call_left, call_signal, interaction_create, interaction_used, component_interaction, interaction_modal, modal_submit, error.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Auth credential in the form `Bearer <token>`, passed as a query param because SSE cannot set request headers

schema.description: SSE stream emitting named events (message_sent, message_edited, message_deleted, member_join, member_leave, member_perms_changed, channel_added, channel_edited, channel_deleted, typing, presence_update, presence_remove, call_start, call_join, call_left, call_signal, interaction_create, interaction_used, component_interaction, interaction_modal, modal_submit, error) plus heartbeat comments

get.description: Returns all slash commands registered by bots that are members of the channel. Includes full option schemas. Use this to build the autocomplete menu when the user types `/`.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Channel the user is viewing.

200.description: All commands from bots in this channel.

404.description: Channel not found or user not a member.

post.description: Submits a slash command interaction. The server validates all option values against the command's schema, then emits an `interaction_create` SSE event to the target bot. The bot receives the caller's identity, the channel, the command name, and the validated options map. Use `GET /channel/{id}/commands` to get option schemas first.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Channel to invoke the command in.

id.description: Interaction ID echoed in the SSE event sent to the bot.

400.description: Missing bot_id or command, option validation failure, or missing required option.

404.description: Channel, bot, or command not found.

post.description: Records a button click on a bot message and emits a `component_interaction` SSE event to the owning bot. The button must exist in the message's components and must not be disabled. Each click creates a new interaction; the bot responds via `POST /interactions/{id}/respond`.

custom_id.description: The custom_id of the button that was clicked.

200.description: Interaction recorded and SSE dispatched to bot.

400.description: Message is not from a bot, or JSON body missing.

404.description: Channel, message, or button not found (or button is disabled).

post.description: Bot-only. Respond to a button-click interaction with either an acknowledgement (`ack`) or a modal form (`modal`). Can only be called once per interaction. A `modal` response sends an `interaction_modal` SSE event to the clicking user, who then fills in the form and submits it.

type.description: ack: silent acknowledgement. modal: open a form on the clicking user's screen.

title.description: Modal heading (max 45 chars). Required when type is modal.

custom_id.description: Echoed back in the modal_submit event.

components.description: Modal action rows (type 1) each containing text input fields (type 4). Required when type is modal.

400.description: Already responded, invalid type, or missing modal fields.

403.description: Not a bot, or not your interaction.

post.description: Called by the client UI when the user submits a modal form. Emits a `modal_submit` SSE event to the bot with a flat map of custom_id → value for each text input field. Only the user who originally clicked the button can submit.

components.description: Flat map of custom_id to submitted value for each text input in the modal.

400.description: Missing or invalid components.

servers.description: Not under /api/v1 — served from the instance root, same as /pfp.

get.description: Streams the raw attachment bytes with `Content-Disposition: attachment`. No Authorization header is required or checked — access control relies entirely on `file_id` being an unguessable id, not on channel membership. If the attachment's `encrypted` flag was true, the bytes are AES-GCM ciphertext: decrypt client-side with the channel AES key referenced by the message's `key` id (see Key distribution) and this attachment's own `iv` (each attachment has its own IV, independent of the message's `iv`). `filename`/`mimetype` are stored and served in the clear even when the bytes are encrypted. Files are deduplicated by content hash server-side, so unrelated messages can share the same `file_id`.

servers.description: Not under /api/v1 — served from the instance root, same as /attachment.

get.description: Streams the raw profile picture bytes (always unencrypted, stored as `.webp`). Uploaded via the `pfp` field on `PATCH /me`. No Authorization header is required or checked.

post.description: Uploads a single image to be referenced from an embed's `image`, `thumbnail`, `author.icon_url`, or `footer.icon_url` field, instead of an arbitrary http(s) URL (which clients refuse to render for privacy: fetching one would leak the viewer's IP to whoever controls that URL). The returned `{id, encrypted, iv}` object is what you put directly in the embed field, e.g. `"image": {"id": "...", "encrypted": true, "iv": "..."}`. In non-broadcast channels, encrypt the image client-side with the channel's current AES key before uploading (`encrypted=1`, `key`=key id, `iv`=IV) the same way message attachments are encrypted. The upload is reserved standalone until a message is actually sent referencing its id via `embed_asset_ids` on POST /channel/{channel_id}/messages; unreferenced uploads are automatically cleaned up after about an hour.

image.description: The image file. Must be an image/* mimetype.

encrypted.description: Pass "1" to mark the upload as client-side encrypted (non-broadcast channels only).

key.description: Current channel AES key id, required when encrypted=1.

400.description: Invalid request

post.description: Adds one reaction row for the caller on this message. Multiple distinct reactions per user per message are allowed (Discord-style); adding the same content twice is rejected with 409 in broadcast channels only — encrypted channels can't be deduped server-side since content is ciphertext with a random IV each time, so clients should avoid offering an already-added emoji again. `content` is plaintext for broadcast channels (type 3); for type 1/2 encrypt it with the channel's current AES key the same way message content is encrypted, and pass `key`/`iv`. `timestamp`/`signature` are required exactly like sending a message, but the signed string is `content:channelId:messageId:signed_timestamp` (note the extra messageId, which message signing doesn't have) so a signature can't be replayed onto a different message.

key.description: Current channel AES key id. Required for channel type 1/2.

iv.description: 16-char AES IV. Required for channel type 1/2.

timestamp.description: Epoch seconds, must be within the server's signature timestamp window.

400.description: Invalid request, reaction limit reached for this message, or message has reached the maximum number of distinct reactions

403.description: No add_reactions permission in this channel

409.description: Already added this exact reaction (broadcast channels only)

delete.description: Removes a reaction by id. Callable by the user who added it, or by anyone with manage_messages permission in the channel (moderator clearing a reaction).

403.description: Not your reaction and no manage_messages permission

404.description: Channel, message, or reaction not found

bearerAuth.description: Authorization: Bearer <token>. A session token (20 chars, from POST /login) or a bot token (30 chars). The /stream endpoint instead takes the value as the ?authorization query argument.

errors.description: Optional per-field rejections on partial-success endpoints.

UserRef.description: Compact user reference embedded in messages/members. Null fields for webhook/broadcast authors.

pfp.description: File id of the profile picture.

content.description: Base64 AES-GCM ciphertext in encrypted channels; plaintext in broadcast (type 3).

key.description: Channel key id used to encrypt this message.

reactions.description: Embedded in every message returned by GET .../messages and in message_sent/message_edited SSE events.

permissions.description: The caller's permission bitfield in this channel.

username.description: For DMs: the other user's username.

public.description: Base64 SPKI RSA public key (392 chars). Returned with ?pb=true.

private.description: If true, only the owner can add/DM it.

key.description: The caller's RSA-OAEP-wrapped copy of the channel AES key (344 chars).

url.description: Relative execution path for this webhook (token appended on send).

CommandOption.description: One typed parameter for a slash command.

type.description: Value type. `user` expects a user ID that is a channel member. `channel` expects a channel ID the caller belongs to.

choices.description: Fixed value list (up to 25). When present the option acts as an enum.

BotCommandSummary.description: A slash command registered by a bot that is a member of the channel.

bot_id.description: ID of the bot that owns the command.

options.description: Map of option name → value. Only include options you want to set; required options must be present.

id.description: File id. Reference this from an embed's image/thumbnail/author.icon_url/footer.icon_url field.

iv.description: 16-char AES IV, present when encrypted is true.

content.description: The emoji. Base64 AES-GCM ciphertext in encrypted channels (type 1/2); plaintext in broadcast (type 3), same split as message content.

key.description: Channel key id used to encrypt this reaction's content.

user.description: Null in broadcast channels for viewers without send_messages/manage_members/manage_permissions, matching message author visibility.
